channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Alexander brothers convicted of sex trafficking in Manhattan federal court
item.description: Three brothers, including two of the nation's most successful luxury real estate brokers, were convicted of sex trafficking Monday after a five-week trial.
item.content:encoded:
Three brothers, including two of the nation's most successful luxury real estate brokers, were convicted of sex trafficking Monday after a five-week trial.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Water restrictions hit home in California
item.description: In California's greatest farming region, there's a water crisis from overpumping groundwater. The state passed a law in 2014 to restrict overdrawing the aquifers, and the limits are going into effect.
item.content:encoded: In California's greatest farming region, there's a water crisis from overpumping groundwater. The state passed a law in 2014 to restrict overdrawing the aquifers, and the limits are going into effect.
item.dc:creator: Dan Charles

item.title: Security wait times at some U.S. airports soar as government shutdown drags on
item.description: Security wait times have ballooned at several airports across the U.S. at Transportation Security Administration checkpoints. Workers are not getting paid as a partial government shutdown drags on.
item.content:encoded: Security wait times have ballooned at several airports across the U.S. at Transportation Security Administration checkpoints. Workers are not getting paid as a partial government shutdown drags on.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Epstein used his ties to Nobel laureate scientists to try to rebuild his image
item.description: A 2006 conference for physicists in the U.S. Virgin Islands that included a trip to Jeffrey Epstein's private island shows how he used his wealth to build relationships with prominent scientists.
item.content:encoded:
A 2006 conference for physicists in the U.S. Virgin Islands that included a trip to Jeffrey Epstein's private island shows how he used his wealth to build relationships with prominent scientists.

(Image credit: JPL-Caltech/NASA, Getty Images and Department of Justice)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Top Arizona lawmaker says he's complied with a subpoena for 2020 election records
item.description: Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed, Republican-led review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona's state Senate president says he has complied with a subpoena he received last week seeking records from a flawed, Republican-led review of the 2020 election in Maricopa County.

(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Swasey

item.title: Anthropic sues the Trump administration over 'supply chain risk' label
item.description: The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.
item.content:encoded:
The Pentagon told suppliers they can't use Anthropic's artificial intelligence tools after the company said it would not let its tech be used for autonomous weapons and mass domestic surveillance.

(Image credit: Eva Marie Uzcategui and Julien de Rosa)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Two suspects charged with aiding ISIS in attempted explosives attack in New York City
item.description: New York City NYPD Commissioner says the explosive devices "could have caused serious injury or death."
item.content:encoded:
New York City NYPD Commissioner says the explosive devices "could have caused serious injury or death."

(Image credit: Joseph B. Frederick)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: The next redistricting battle might be who is counted in state legislative districts
item.description: A Republican push to alter the census may lead to a radical shift in redistricting for state legislatures — drawing districts that don't take into account children and non-U.S. citizen adults.
item.content:encoded:
A Republican push to alter the census may lead to a radical shift in redistricting for state legislatures — drawing districts that don't take into account children and non-U.S. citizen adults.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Improvised explosive device thrown outside Mamdani's New York residence
item.description: Police say a device thrown during an anti-Muslim protest outside New York City's Gracie Mansion was an improvised explosive device. Federal investigators are now involved.
item.content:encoded: Police say a device thrown during an anti-Muslim protest outside New York City's Gracie Mansion was an improvised explosive device. Federal investigators are now involved.
item.dc:creator: Catalina Gonella

item.title: U.S. military kills 6 in strike on alleged drug boat in the Eastern Pacific
item.description: Sunday's attack brought the death toll to at least 157 people since the Trump administration began targeting alleged drug-smuggling vessels, in early September.
item.content:encoded:
Sunday's attack brought the death toll to at least 157 people since the Trump administration began targeting alleged drug-smuggling vessels, in early September.

(Image credit: Konstantin Toropin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
